data.title: UK Treasury chief heading to China to revive strained ties
data.description:
Britain's Chancellor Rachel Reeves is heading to China this weekend to revive the China-UK Economic and Financial Dialogue—annual bilateral talks suspended since 2019 due to the pandemic and deteriorating relations.

The three-day trip includes meetings in Beijing and Shanghai, where she will engage with her Chinese counterpart, Vice Premier He Lifeng. She will be accompanied by other financial leaders, including HSBC's (HSBC) Chair Mark Tucker, Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey, and the U.K.’s financial watchdog, Nikhil Rathi, in a low-profile visit with no press accompanying.

This diplomatic effort follows recent engagements by other UK officials, including Foreign Secretary David Lammy's visit in October and Prime Minister Keir Starmer's meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping during the G20 summit in Brazil.

The visit comes amid rising instability over the UK's domestic economic challenges, particularly concerning increasing government borrowing costs and a depreciation of the British pound.

The pound (GBY:USD) (USD:GBY) plummeted to its lowest level in more than a year, trading at $1.2304 at 12:00 p.m. London time, piling more pressure on the Labour Party regarding their fiscal plans.

The Conservatives and Liberal Democrats have criticized the chancellor for the trip rather than staying in the UK to address domestic economic turbulence. Shadow chancellor Mel Stride accused Reeves of being "missing in action," BBC reported.

"the US, markets are taking a different view of how their economy is going to go once President Trump is in office and their rates have gone up to 4.75% on 10-year Treasuries and ours have gone up in parallel to that. So I don't think this is a response to something we've done," Sir John Gieve, former deputy governor of the Bank of England, told BBC.

data.title: Constellation Energy confirms $26.6B deal for Calpine
data.description:
Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) +5.2% pre-market Friday after saying it agreed to acquire Calpine in a cash and stock deal valued at $26.6B, composed of 50M Constellation shares and $4.5B in cash plus the assumption of $12.7B of Calpine net debt.

Calpine is the largest U.S. producer of energy from low-emission natural gas generation and an expanded renewable energy portfolio, and Constellation (CEG) said the acquisition will create the largest U.S. clean energy provider as well as the country's leading competitive retail electric supplier, providing 2.5M customers with a broader array of customized energy and sustainability solutions.

Constellation (CEG) said the deal will bring immediate adjusted operating earnings per share accretion of more than 20% in 2026 and at least $2/share of EPS accretion in future years, and is projected to add more than $2B non-GAAP of free cash flow annually.

data.title: NeoGenomics CEO Chris Smith to retire
data.description:
• NeoGenomics (NASDAQ:NEO) said its CEO, Chris Smith, will retire from the role, effective April 1.
  • The company added Tony Zook, an independent board member since 2023, will assume the role of CEO.
  • Smith was named CEO in August 2022.
  • The company also reaffirmed its full-year 2024 guidance.

data.title: Stock index futures lower as investors look to key jobs report
data.description:
Stock index futures reversed course and turned red on Friday, as investors braced for the key December jobs report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.70%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.29%.

U.S. markets were shut on Thursday for a national day of mourning for former U.S. President Jimmy Carter.

The next test for markets will now be the U.S. jobs report for December, Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"Indeed, it’s worth noting that one of the catalysts for this week’s selloff was the upside surprise in the 'prices paid' component of the ISM services index on Tuesday. So another upside surprise in payrolls would give further momentum to the idea that the Fed should be cautious about cutting rates from here," Allen added.

Economists expect the U.S. economy will add 157K jobs to nonfarm payrolls in December, slowing from the initial November estimate of 227K. Meanwhile, the unemployment rate is expected to remain at 4.2%.

"The payrolls number is derived from a survey that has a 43% response rate, and that poor representation means significant revisions are much more likely. The market is looking for a fairly dull report overall—some moderation in job creation with a stable unemployment rate," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The January consumer sentiment is also expected to land during market hours, and is forecasted to come in at 74.

U.S. Michigan consumer sentiment is still vulnerable to partisan bias. The recent improvement is mainly due to surging Republican sentiment. Republicans are more emotional than Democrats, pushing the overall index higher. Partisan bias does affect the inflation expectations component, Donovan added.

data.title: Falcon's Beyond Global files for $100M class A shares offering
data.description:

  • FBYD shares were trading -11.94% Friday pre-market at $6.05.

data.title: Astronics guides Q4 revenue above consensus, shares rise
data.description:
Astronics (NASDAQ:ATRO) has reported preliminary Q4 revenue in the range of $208M to $210M (consensus estimate: $194.91M), which was the upper end of the company’s guidance range.

Preliminary bookings in the quarter were $199M, bringing orders for the full year to ~$811M. Full year 2024 preliminary unaudited revenue is estimated at ~$796M ($781.80M estimate), an increase of 15.5% over 2023.

The company also set preliminary revenue expectations for 2025 of $820M to $860M ($836.39M estimate), representing an increase of ~6% over 2024 at the mid-point of the range.

Peter Gundermann, chairman, president and CEO, commented, “We ended the year on a strong note despite sluggish OEM production rates and the Boeing strike. We have achieved average annual revenue growth of over 20% the last three years and have finally returned to pre-Covid revenue levels. We expect 2025 will be another year of solid growth, though more modest, which will allow us to focus on expanding margins through the year and strengthening cash flow.”

Astronics (NASDAQ:ATRO) added that deliveries to Boeing following the end of the strike have been slowly resuming at a relatively low rate.

Shares of Astronics (ATRO) rose over 6.35% in premarket trading.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Delta Air Lines leads the airline sector after issuing a strong 2025 earnings forecast
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) fell in early trading on Friday after the airline company reported revenue of $15.56 billion to top the consensus estimate by $1.08 billion. Earnings per share came in at $1.85, vs. $1.74 consensus and $1.28 a year ago.

The airline company noted that demand accelerated through the quarter. During the months of November and December, Delta (NYSE:DAL) saw four of the top ten revenue days in the company's history and double-digit growth in cash bookings, driven by both leisure and corporate travelers. Notably, corporate sales were up by a double-digit rate during the quarter.

All three of Delta's (NYSE:DAL) international geographies improved sequentially and relative to initial expectations, with international passenger revenue growth of 6% year-over-year in the December quarter.

Delta (DAL) reported an adjusted operating margin of 12.0% for the quarter, vs. 9.9% a year ago as lower fuel costs factored in. Total revenue per available seat mile was $0.2004 vs. $0.1995 a year ago, while adjusted cost per available seat mile was $0.1372 vs. $0.1329 a year ago.

"As we move into 2025, we expect strong demand for travel to continue, with consumers increasingly seeking the premium products and experiences that Delta provides," highlighted CEO Ed Bastian. "Our differentiated strategy and best-in-class operations, combined with demand strength and an increasingly constructive industry backdrop, position us to deliver the best financial year in Delta's 100-year history, with pre-tax income greater than $6 billion, earnings per share greater than $7.35 and free cash flow of more than $4 billion," he added.

On the guidance front, Delta Air Lines (DAL) sees Q1 EPS of $0.70 to $1.00 (midpoint $0.85) vs. $0.76 consensus and 2025 adjusted EPS above $7.35 vs. $7.45 consensus.

Shares of Delta Air Lines (DAL) rose 3.35% in premarket trading. Across the airline sector, American Airlines Group (AAL), United Airlines (UAL), JetBlue Airways (JBLU), and Southwest Airlines (LUV) also showed gains.
